mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending power, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects fading capacity utilization and weakening manufacturing demand, leading to negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or INDPRO declines coincide with positive SPX returns in more than 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and consumer credit, causing immediate downward pressure on equity prices within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms sustainable job creation, supporting wage growth and consumer confidence, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate increased construction activity and household wealth effects, which flow through to corporate revenues and produce positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or the series exhibits zero rank correlation with forward returns over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production below 110 during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks and weak demand, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 110 during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests.
